question: [File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker grief a victim by submitting a valid authorization that grants authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.repay(market, 1 wei, victim, ...) repeatedly, under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and victim has an active debt position, triggering the call sequence operator grants dustRepayor via EcrecoverAuthorizer, dustRepayor calls Midnight.repay(market, 1 wei, victim, ...) N times, consuming victim's debt in dust increments and potentially triggering rounding losses on each repayment, violating the invariant that dust repayments must not cause rounding-based accounting losses for the victim, causing scoped impact: griefing of victim's debt accounting through dust repayment rounding? Proof idea: fuzz test with repay amounts in {1, 2, dust-1, dust} and assert total repaid == total debt reduction.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an authorized operator use EcrecoverAuthorizer to grant authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.withdraw(market, 1 wei, victim, ...) repeatedly, under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and victim has credit in a market, triggering the call sequence operator grants dustWithdrawer via EcrecoverAuthorizer, dustWithdrawer calls Midnight.withdraw(market, 1 wei, victim, ...) N times, consuming victim's credit in dust increments and potentially triggering rounding losses on each withdrawal, violating the invariant that dust withdrawals must not cause rounding-based accounting losses for the victim, causing scoped impact: griefing of victim's credit accounting through dust withdrawal rounding? Proof idea: fuzz test with withdraw amounts in {1, 2, dust-1, dust} and assert total withdrawn == total credit reduction.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker grief a victim by submitting a valid authorization that grants authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.supplyCollateral(market, 0, 1 wei, victim) to deposit dust collateral, under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and victim has no collateral in slot 0, triggering the call sequence operator grants dustDepositor via EcrecoverAuthorizer, dustDepositor calls Midnight.supplyCollateral(market, 0, 1 wei, victim), setting collateralBitmap bit 0 for victim, then victim cannot withdraw this dust collateral without triggering health checks, violating the invariant that dust collateral deposits must not create irremovable bitmap entries, causing scoped impact: griefing of victim's collateral bitmap state through dust deposits? Proof idea: unit test asserting that 1 wei collateral deposit sets bitmap bit and victim cannot withdraw without health check.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an authorized operator use EcrecoverAuthorizer to grant authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.setConsumed(group, amount, victim) to advance victim's offer consumption to exactly maxUnits-1, under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and victim has an active offer with maxUnits capacity, triggering the call sequence operator grants consumptionManipulator via EcrecoverAuthorizer, consumptionManipulator calls Midnight.setConsumed(group, maxUnits-1, victim), leaving only 1 unit of capacity, then a taker can only fill 1 unit, violating the invariant that offer consumption state must reflect actual fills and not be manipulable by authorized delegates, causing scoped impact: griefing of victim's offer capacity through partial consumption manipulation? Proof idea: unit test asserting consumptionManipulator can advance victim's consumed to maxUnits-1 and subsequent takes are limited to 1 unit.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker grief a victim by submitting a valid authorization with authorization.nonce == nonce[victim] but with authorization.isAuthorized=false for an address the victim has not yet authorized, under the precondition that victim has nonce=N and has signed a revocation for a future operator, triggering the call sequence attacker submits victim's signed revocation for futureOperator with nonce=N, consuming nonce N, so victim's intended grant authorization with nonce=N fails InvalidNonce, violating the invariant that revocation authorizations must not be submittable before the corresponding grant, causing scoped impact: griefing of victim's authorization sequence? Proof idea: unit test asserting that a revocation auth with nonce=N can be submitted before the grant auth with nonce=N.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an authorized operator use EcrecoverAuthorizer to grant authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.liquidate on behalf of the victim as the liquidator, under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and a market has a liquidatorGate that allows victim but not the malicious contract, triggering the call sequence operator grants maliciousLiquidator via EcrecoverAuthorizer, maliciousLiquidator calls Midnight.liquidate(market, ..., borrower, ...) on behalf of victim, but the liquidatorGate checks msg.sender (maliciousLiquidator) not victim, violating the invariant that liquidator gate must check the actual caller not the authorized principal, causing scoped impact: griefing of victim's liquidation rights or bypass of liquidator gate? Proof idea: integration test with liquidatorGate checking msg.sender, assert maliciousLiquidator can or cannot liquidate after being authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker grief a victim by submitting a valid authorization that grants authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.flashLoan with victim's collateral as the callback receiver, under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and victim has collateral deposited, triggering the call sequence operator grants flashLoanAbuser via EcrecoverAuthorizer, flashLoanAbuser calls Midnight.flashLoan([collateralToken], [amount], flashLoanAbuser, data) where the callback withdraws victim's collateral, violating the invariant that flash loan callbacks must not be able to access victim's collateral without explicit victim authorization, causing scoped impact: temporary freezing or seizure of victim's collateral via flash loan callback? Proof idea: integration test where flashLoanAbuser withdraws victim's collateral during a flash loan callback after being authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an authorized operator use EcrecoverAuthorizer to grant authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.take with victim as the taker (seller), increasing victim's debt without victim's knowledge, under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and victim has collateral deposited, triggering the call sequence operator grants debtIncreaser via EcrecoverAuthorizer, debtIncreaser calls Midnight.take(offer, ..., victim, ...) where victim is the seller, increasing victim's debt, violating the invariant that debt increases must require explicit victim authorization at the time of the take, causing scoped impact: temporary freezing of victim's collateral through unauthorized debt increase? Proof idea: integration test where debtIncreaser takes on behalf of victim as seller, assert victim's debt increases.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker grief a victim by submitting a valid authorization with authorization.authorized == MIDNIGHT (the Midnight contract address), under the precondition that victim signed such an authorization, triggering the call sequence attacker submits Authorization{authorized=MIDNIGHT, isAuthorized=true, nonce=N} via EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized, setting isAuthorized[victim][MIDNIGHT] = true, then Midnight itself (as msg.sender in a callback) could call Midnight.setIsAuthorized or other functions on behalf of victim, violating the invariant that the Midnight contract must not be authorizable as an operator for users, causing scoped impact: griefing of victim's authorization state through Midnight-as-operator? Proof idea: unit test asserting that authorizing MIDNIGHT as an operator has no exploitable effect.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an authorized operator use EcrecoverAuthorizer to grant authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.updatePosition(market, victim) to crystallize pending fees against victim, under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and victim has accrued continuous fees, triggering the call sequence operator grants feeGriever via EcrecoverAuthorizer, feeGriever calls Midnight.updatePosition(market, victim) repeatedly, crystallizing continuous fees and reducing victim's credit through rounding losses on each update, violating the invariant that fee crystallization must not cause rounding-based credit losses beyond documented bounds, causing scoped impact: griefing of victim's credit through repeated fee crystallization? Proof idea: fuzz test with N calls to updatePosition and assert total credit reduction == expected fee accrual within rounding bounds.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker grief a victim by submitting a valid authorization that grants authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.setConsumed(group, 0, victim) to reset consumed state, under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and victim has partially consumed offers, triggering the call sequence operator grants consumptionResetter via EcrecoverAuthorizer, consumptionResetter calls Midnight.setConsumed(group, 0, victim), but this should revert with AlreadyConsumed() if consumed[victim][group] > 0, violating the invariant that consumed state must be monotonically increasing, causing scoped impact: confirming that partial-fill state cannot be reset by authorized delegates? Proof idea: unit test asserting Midnight.setConsumed(group, 0, victim) reverts AlreadyConsumed when consumed[victim][group] > 0.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an authorized operator use EcrecoverAuthorizer to grant authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.withdrawCollateral(market, i, type(uint128).max, victim, attacker) for each collateral index i, under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and victim has collateral deposited, triggering the call sequence operator grants collateralDrainer via EcrecoverAuthorizer, collateralDrainer calls Midnight.withdrawCollateral for each collateral index, draining victim's collateral to attacker, violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal must require explicit victim authorization at the time of withdrawal, causing scoped impact: seizure of victim's collateral through authorized delegate? Proof idea: integration test where collateralDrainer drains victim's collateral after being authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker grief a victim by submitting a valid authorization with authorization.nonce == nonce[victim] where nonce[victim] is near type(uint256).max, under the precondition that victim has used EcrecoverAuthorizer type(uint256).max times (practically impossible but worth bounding), triggering the call sequence nonce[victim]++ overflows in Solidity 0.8.34 and reverts, permanently freezing victim's ability to use EcrecoverAuthorizer, violating the invariant that nonce overflow must not permanently freeze authorization, causing scoped impact: permanent freezing of victim's EcrecoverAuthorizer usage? Proof idea: unit test with vm.store to set nonce[victim] = type(uint256).max and assert the next setIsAuthorized call reverts with overflow.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an authorized operator use EcrecoverAuthorizer to grant authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.take with victim as the buyer, increasing victim's credit and triggering continuous fee accrual, under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and a market has a non-zero continuous fee, triggering the call sequence operator grants creditIncreaser via EcrecoverAuthorizer, creditIncreaser calls Midnight.take(offer, ..., victim, ...) where victim is the buyer, increasing victim's credit and starting continuous fee accrual, violating the invariant that credit increases must require explicit victim authorization at the time of the take, causing scoped impact: griefing of victim through unauthorized credit increase and continuous fee obligation? Proof idea: integration test where creditIncreaser takes on behalf of victim as buyer, assert victim's credit increases and continuous fee accrues.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker grief a victim by submitting a valid authorization that grants authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.setIsAuthorized(EcrecoverAuthorizer, true, victim) to re-authorize EcrecoverAuthorizer after it was revoked, under the precondition that victim has revoked EcrecoverAuthorizer's authorization on Midnight and the attacker has obtained a signed authorization for this re-grant, triggering the call sequence attacker submits victim's signed Authorization{authorized=EcrecoverAuthorizer, isAuthorized=true, nonce=N} via EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized, but this requires EcrecoverAuthorizer to already be authorized on Midnight for victim (circular dependency), violating the invariant that a revoked EcrecoverAuthorizer cannot re-authorize itself, causing scoped impact: confirming that EcrecoverAuthorizer cannot bootstrap its own authorization? Proof idea: unit test asserting that EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized reverts when EcrecoverAuthorizer is not authorized on Midnight for victim.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an authorized operator use EcrecoverAuthorizer to grant authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.repay(market, type(uint128).max, victim, ...) to fully repay victim's debt, under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and victim has an active debt position with collateral, triggering the call sequence operator grants debtRepayor via EcrecoverAuthorizer, debtRepayor calls Midnight.repay(market, type(uint128).max, victim, ...) fully repaying victim's debt, then victim's collateral is freed but debtRepayor controls the repayment timing, violating the invariant that debt repayment must reflect borrower intent, causing scoped impact: griefing of victim's debt management strategy through unauthorized full repayment? Proof idea: unit test where debtRepayor fully repays victim's debt after being authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker grief a victim by submitting a valid authorization with authorization.authorized == authorization.authorizer == attacker (attacker authorizing themselves), under the precondition that attacker has signed an authorization granting themselves access to their own account, triggering the call sequence attacker submits Authorization{authorizer=attacker, authorized=attacker, isAuthorized=true, nonce=0} via EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized, consuming attacker's nonce 0, then attacker's intended authorization with nonce=0 for a different address fails InvalidNonce, violating the invariant that self-authorization must not consume nonces in a way that blocks other pending authorizations, causing scoped impact: self-griefing of attacker's authorization queue? Proof idea: unit test asserting self-authorization consumes nonce and subsequent authorization with same nonce reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an authorized operator use EcrecoverAuthorizer to grant authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.multicall to batch multiple state changes against victim in a single transaction, under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and victim has active positions, triggering the call sequence operator grants multicallAbuser via EcrecoverAuthorizer, multicallAbuser calls Midnight.multicall([withdrawCollateral(..., victim, ...), repay(..., victim, ...), setIsAuthorized(..., victim)]) atomically, violating the invariant that multicall must not allow authorized delegates to batch state changes that individually would be detectable but together cause unexpected state, causing scoped impact: griefing of victim through atomic multicall state manipulation? Proof idea: integration test where multicallAbuser batches collateral withdrawal and debt repayment for victim in a single multicall.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker grief a victim by submitting a valid authorization with a deadline that is exactly one second before market maturity, under the precondition that victim signed an authorization with deadline == market.maturity - 1 and the attacker submits it at block.timestamp == market.maturity - 1, triggering the call sequence attacker calls EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized at block.timestamp == market.maturity - 1, which passes the deadline check, consuming nonce N, and the resulting authorization change affects victim's ability to repay or withdraw at maturity, violating the invariant that pre-maturity authorization changes must not interfere with maturity settlement flows, causing scoped impact: temporary freezing of victim's maturity settlement? Proof idea: state test with vm.warp(market.maturity - 1) and assert authorization change affects repay/withdraw at maturity.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an authorized operator use EcrecoverAuthorizer to grant authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.withdrawCollateral(market, i, amount, victim, victim) for a collateral index where victim has dust collateral (1 wei), under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and victim has dust collateral in slot i, triggering the call sequence operator grants dustWithdrawer via EcrecoverAuthorizer, dustWithdrawer calls Midnight.withdrawCollateral(market, i, 1 wei, victim, victim), clearing the bitmap bit for slot i, then victim's health check no longer includes this collateral, potentially making victim's position unhealthy, violating the invariant that collateral bitmap must accurately reflect deposited collateral, causing scoped impact: griefing of victim's health through dust collateral withdrawal? Proof idea: unit test asserting that withdrawing 1 wei collateral clears bitmap bit and affects health check.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker grief a victim by submitting a valid authorization that grants authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.supplyCollateral(market, i, amount, victim) for a collateral index i that is not in victim's current bitmap, under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and victim's bitmap is at MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER - 1, triggering the call sequence operator grants collateralAdder via EcrecoverAuthorizer, collateralAdder calls Midnight.supplyCollateral(market, MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER - 1, 1 wei, victim), filling victim's bitmap to MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER, then victim cannot add any more collateral types, violating the invariant that bitmap limit must not be reachable by authorized delegates without victim's explicit consent, causing scoped impact: griefing of victim's collateral diversification through bitmap limit? Proof idea: unit test asserting that after bitmap is full, victim cannot add a new collateral type.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an authorized operator use EcrecoverAuthorizer to grant authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.take with victim as the seller at a tick that is not a multiple of the market's tickSpacing, under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and the market has a non-default tickSpacing, triggering the call sequence operator grants invalidTickTaker via EcrecoverAuthorizer, invalidTickTaker calls Midnight.take(offer, ..., victim, ...) where offer.tick is not a multiple of tickSpacing, which should revert with TickNotAccessible, violating the invariant that tick spacing restrictions must apply to all callers including authorized delegates, causing scoped impact: confirming that tick spacing restrictions cannot be bypassed through authorization? Proof idea: unit test asserting that take with invalid tick reverts TickNotAccessible even when called by an authorized delegate.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker grief a victim by submitting a valid authorization that grants authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.take with victim as the seller after market maturity, under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and the market has passed maturity, triggering the call sequence operator grants postMaturityTaker via EcrecoverAuthorizer, postMaturityTaker calls Midnight.take(offer, ..., victim, ...) where victim is the seller after maturity, which should revert if debt increase is forbidden post-maturity, violating the invariant that debt must not increase after maturity even through authorized delegates, causing scoped impact: confirming that post-maturity debt increase restrictions apply to authorized delegates? Proof idea: state test with vm.warp(market.maturity + 1) and assert take as seller reverts for victim.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an authorized operator use EcrecoverAuthorizer to grant authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.liquidate on behalf of victim as the liquidator, seizing victim's own collateral, under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and victim has an unhealthy position, triggering the call sequence operator grants selfLiquidator via EcrecoverAuthorizer, selfLiquidator calls Midnight.liquidate(market, 0, seizedAssets, repaidUnits, victim, false, selfLiquidator, ...) where selfLiquidator is the receiver, violating the invariant that liquidation must not be usable by authorized delegates to self-liquidate at favorable terms, causing scoped impact: griefing of victim through unauthorized self-liquidation? Proof idea: integration test where selfLiquidator liquidates victim's position after being authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer.

[File: 'src/periphery/EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker grief a victim by submitting a valid authorization that grants authorization to a contract that calls Midnight.claimContinuousFee(market, amount, attacker) on behalf of the fee claimer role, under the precondition that operator is authorized on Midnight for victim and victim is the fee claimer, triggering the call sequence operator grants feeDrainer via EcrecoverAuthorizer, feeDrainer calls Midnight.claimContinuousFee(market, amount, attacker), draining victim's claimable fees to attacker, violating the invariant that fee claims must require explicit fee claimer authorization, causing scoped impact: griefing of victim's fee revenue through unauthorized fee claim? Proof idea: unit test asserting that feeDrainer can claim victim's fees after being authorized via EcrecoverAuthorizer if victim is the fee claimer.
